addMarketWithLine(bytes32,int16).details: `line` semantics depend on the market type. Examples:           - O/U markets: line in 1/10 goal/point units (25 = 2.5)           - Scalar/bucket markets: line = cap of the highest bucket           - Markets that ignore the line: pass 0

returns.resolvable: False if this market type cannot be derived from `s`                     in its current configuration (caller should skip).

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

placeBetUSDC(uint256,uint64,uint256).details: Caller must approve `amount` USDC to THIS contract.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

resolveByScore((uint8,uint8,uint8,uint8,.details: Markets that cannot be derived from `s` (e.g. FIRST_SCORER when         `firstScorerId == 0`) are skipped — they remain Closed and the         oracle may settle them later via `resolveMarket`.         Markets in any state other than Closed are skipped.         Reverts only on access control / arithmetic errors.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

MAX_FEE_BPS().notice: Hard ceiling on the protocol fee (5%). Prevents fee parameter         from being raised to a level that would constitute a hidden         expropriation of user funds.

MIN_STAKE().notice: Minimum stake per placement (anti-dust / anti-griefing).         0.01 USDC in 6-decimal precision.

addMarketAdvanced(bytes32,int16,uint8,ui.notice: Add a market with sport-specific `extra` byte (e.g. basketball         quarter) and optional UI `groupId`.

addMarketWithLine(bytes32,int16).notice: Add a market of `marketType` with optional `line` parameter.

addMarketsBatch(bytes32[],int16[]).notice: Batch add markets sharing default extra/groupId = 0.

addMarketsBatchAdvanced(bytes32[],int16[.notice: Batch add markets with sport-specific `extra` and `groupId`.

computeOutcome(uint256,(uint8,uint8,uint.notice: View helper: what outcome would `s` produce for `marketId`?

feeBps().notice: TOTAL protocol fee in basis points (default 200 = 2%).         Split between company and leaderboard at resolve time:           leaderboardFee = totalPool * leaderboardFeeBps / BPS_DENOM           companyFee     = totalPool * (feeBps - leaderboardFeeBps) / BPS_DENOM         Capped at MAX_FEE_BPS. The setter enforces         `leaderboardFeeBps <= feeBps` so the split is always valid.

feeRecipient().notice: Address that receives the company portion of the protocol         fee at market resolution. Should be a multi-sig or treasury.

getImpliedProbabilityBps(uint256,uint64).notice: Off-chain indicative implied probability for an outcome.

leaderboardFeeBps().notice: Leaderboard's share of the total fee, in basis points OF THE         POOL. E.g. feeBps=200, leaderboardFeeBps=100 → 1% of pool to         leaderboard, 1% of pool to company. Must satisfy         `leaderboardFeeBps <= feeBps`.

leaderboardRecipient().notice: Address that receives the leaderboard portion of the protocol         fee at market resolution. Expected to be a         `LeaderboardRewards` proxy. May be zero — then the full fee         goes to `feeRecipient` and no `recordWin` notify happens.

marketCount().notice: Total number of markets created on this match.

matchName().notice: Human-readable match identifier (e.g. "Barcelona vs Real Madrid").

placeBetUSDC(uint256,uint64,uint256).notice: Place a stake directly with USDC.

placeBetUSDCFor(address,uint256,uint64,u.notice: Place a stake on behalf of a user after the router has already         transferred `amount` USDC to this contract.

resolveBatchByScore(uint256[],(uint8,uin.notice: Resolve a chosen subset of markets from a final-score input.         Useful when the oracle wants per-market control or knows that         some markets in this batch are not derivable from `s`.

resolveByScore((uint8,uint8,uint8,uint8,.notice: Resolve every Closed market of this match from one final-score input.

setLeaderboardFeeBps(uint16).notice: Configure the leaderboard portion of the total fee. Must be         <= `feeBps`. Zero means "no leaderboard split — full fee to         company" (backward-compatible default).

setLeaderboardRecipient(address).notice: Set the address that receives the leaderboard share of the         fee. Zero clears the recipient (no leaderboard payouts; the         `recordWin` notify in `_processClaim` becomes a no-op).         When non-zero, must be the address of a `LeaderboardRewards`         proxy (or compatible `ILeaderboardRewards` sink).

sportType().notice: Sport identifier set by the child initializer.

usdcToken().notice: USDC token used for all settlements.
